overrides.override_summary: Venue/arbitration provisions requiring Dallas TX may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to litigate or arbitrate in California rather than traveling to Dallas, TX

overrides.override_summary: Texas law choice may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchise investment law and other CA statutes may apply instead of Texas law

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete provisions may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California generally prohibits noncompete agreements; franchisee may be free to operate competing business post-term

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under California Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: The $500,000 minimum and formula-based liquidated damages may not be collectible in CA

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal cannot waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law (Corporations Code Sections 31000-31516)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees retain all statutory protections even upon renewal

overrides.override_summary: Maximum interest rate capped at 10% annually under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: The FA's 2%/month (24% annual) interest rate is reduced to 10% in CA

overrides.override_summary: Any provision requiring franchisee to waive rights under Franchise Investment Law is void and unenforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor cannot disclaim representations or deny franchisee's ability to rely on FDD statements

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Automatic termination upon bankruptcy filing may be stayed or voided under 11 U.S.C.

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of claims under state franchise law including fraud in the inducement; no disclaimer of reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's right to bring fraud claims

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act of 1987 (815 ILCS 705) governs the franchise relationship
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law supersedes Texas law for IL franchisees

overrides.override_summary: IL law may override Dallas TX venue requirement
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees may have right to litigate in Illinois

overrides.override_summary: IC 23-2-2.7-1(7) prohibits unilateral termination without good cause or in bad faith; good cause = material breach
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's termination rights to material breaches only

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete modified to comply with IC 23-2-2.7-1(9)
overrides.why_it_matters: Indiana may limit the scope and enforceability of noncompete provisions

overrides.override_summary: Prospective assent to release/waiver/novation that relieves person from liability under Indiana Deceptive Franchise Practices Law is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive Indiana franchise law protections in advance

overrides.override_summary: Any provision limiting litigation for breach of agreement is void if it violates Indiana Deceptive Franchise Practices Law
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees retain full litigation rights under state law

overrides.override_summary: General release not required as condition of renewal, sale, transfer, or assignment where it would release franchisor from liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees cannot be forced to release statutory claims on renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: Provisions requiring franchisee to assent to release or waiver of claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law are not enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves all statutory protections for MD franchisees

overrides.override_summary: MN franchise laws supersede FA/ADA provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota statutes provide additional protections beyond Texas law

overrides.override_summary: General release not required as condition of renewal or transfer
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain right to assert claims under MN law upon renewal

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete restrictions limited and may not be enforceable beyond reasonable scope under MN law
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete may be narrowed in MN

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed location; litigation may be brought in WA for franchise sale or FIPA violation
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees can resolve disputes locally rather than in Dallas TX

overrides.override_summary: Initial franchise fees deferred until franchisee has received all pre-opening training obligations and is open for business
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees do not pay the $50,000 franchise fee until after training and opening — significantly reduces pre-opening cash burden

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void against employees earning <$100K/year (adjusted for inflation); void against independent contractors earning <$250K/year per RCW 49.62
overrides.why_it_matters: Salary thresholds may render noncompete unenforceable against lower-earning franchisee employees or contractors

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver may not include rights under WA FIPA except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees retain statutory rights through franchise lifecycle

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees collectible only to extent reflecting franchisor's reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: The flat $12,500 transfer fee may be challenged if it exceeds actual costs in WA
